components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: test strains were cultured in M63 minimal media [29] with 0.5% (w/v) glycerol, 0.5% (w/v) L-fucose, or 0.5% (w/v) glucose as the carbon source.

components.name: ammonium sulfate ((NH4)2SO4)
components.evidence: The 10x M63 salts solution contained 15 mM of (NH4)2SO4, 100 mM of KH2PO4, and 2 x 10-2 mM of FeSO4.7H2O.

components.name: monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.evidence: The 10x M63 salts solution contained 15 mM of (NH4)2SO4, 100 mM of KH2PO4, and 2 x 10-2 mM of FeSO4.7H2O.

components.name: ferrous sulfate heptahydrate (FeSO4.7H2O)
components.evidence: The 10x M63 salts solution contained 15 mM of (NH4)2SO4, 100 mM of KH2PO4, and 2 x 10-2 mM of FeSO4.7H2O.

components.name: magnesium sulfate (MgSO4)
components.evidence: After diluting to 1x M63 medium, it was supplemented with 10-4% thiamine and 1.7 mM MgSO4.

components.name: thiamine (vitamin B1)
components.evidence: After diluting to 1x M63 medium, it was supplemented with 10-4% thiamine and 1.7 mM MgSO4.

components.name: water
components.evidence: M63 minimal media [29] ... After diluting to 1x M63 medium

components.name: dioxygen (O2)
components.evidence: Bacterial strains were routinely grown in LB media at 30 C or 37 C ... cultured in M63 minimal media (aerobic shake cultures for fucAO/lacZ reporter assays)

components.name: proton (H+)
components.evidence: M63 is a phosphate-buffered defined medium adjusted to ~pH 7 with KOH (classic M63 recipe, ref 29).

media.base_medium: M63 minimal medium (Pardee, Jacob & Monod 1959 / Miller)

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as ref 29 (Pardee AB, Jacob F, Monod J. J Mol Biol 1959;1:165-78) — origin of 'medium 63' (M63)
sources_chased.how: identified from reference list; classic M63 composition confirmed no additional trace-metal or vitamin mix beyond iron and thiamine
added: confirmed medium identity; no hidden trace/vitamin stock

media.notes: M63 is a fully defined minimal medium; the paper's methods spell out the entire composition (NH4/SO4, KH2PO4 phosphate buffer, Fe, Mg, thiamine, carbon source) and classic M63 (ref 29) adds no trace-metal or vitamin mix, so nothing was hidden. Caveat: the paper labels the salts as a '10x' stock yet lists 15 mM (NH4)2SO4 and 100 mM KH2PO4 which already equal standard 1x M63 working concentrations; conc_mM therefore reports these as the working (1x) values (the FeSO4 value, given as 2e-2 mM in 10x, dilutes to ~2e-3 mM = standard M63 1x iron). Ampicillin/kanamycin/chloramphenicol were added only 'when necessary' for selection and are not part of the growth medium.

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as ref 29 (Pardee AB, Jacob F, Monod J. J Mol Biol 1959;1:165-78) — origin of 'medium 63' (M63)
sources_chased.how: identified from reference list; classic M63 composition confirmed no additional trace-metal or vitamin mix beyond iron and thiamine
added: confirmed medium identity; no hidden trace/vitamin stock

media.notes: Same defined M63 base as the glucose/glycerol media, differing only in carbon source (0.5% w/v L-fucose = ~30.5 mM). The paper studies L-fucose metabolism both aerobically and anaerobically, but the M63 fucAO/lacZ reporter assays are aerobic shake cultures. Fully defined; no hidden trace/vitamin mix. Same '10x' salts-label caveat as the other two media.
